description_long: This bicycle repair station features a robust wooden base supporting a series of metal arms and levers, each painted in distinct colors for easy identification. The structure includes three main angled supports, each anchored securely to the base with visible bolts. A prominent handle is attached to one of the arms, likely used to operate a mechanical function such as a clamp or lift. The colored levers and connecting rods suggest multiple interactive components, possibly for adjusting or securing different parts of a bicycle. The overall design is compact and functional, with all moving parts exposed for easy access and maintenance. The station is intended for public or semi-public urban environments, providing essential repair capabilities for cyclists.
description: A compact bicycle repair station with a wooden base, colorful metal arms, and interactive levers for securing and servicing bicycles. The exposed mechanical components allow for easy operation and maintenance.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three main angled supports, colorful levers, and the wooden base with visible bolts.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the prominent handle and the alignment of the arms and levers along the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the angled supports and the arrangement of the colored levers from behind.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the handle and the mechanical linkage between the arms.
